EI\,IAIL PL\YERS C OMI\,IAI\DMENT I
Thou Shalt Write At LEAST 20 Articles
And/Or Ernails Per Day!
Yes, for the next 30 days, each day you must write more copy than you ever have in
your life. A true story: Back in early 2011 I wanted to build a side income based off article
marketing. The idea was to write close to 1,000 short (250-350 words) ezine articles (for
ezinearticles.com and a blog), SEO'd just right, and then let that generate tons of trafñc
for an ebook I wrote. This worked for one of my business partners (he basicalty wrote
1200 articles over B monüs for the weight loss niche, and for 2 years dragged in a $70k
per year business doing literally nothing -- and without affiliates, back end sales, etc). Too
bad I didn't get the time travel memo that Google would do their article farm slap about
a week or two after I got all those articles up. (All that work for nada.) Ary*uy, it was
during that time when I forced myself to write 15 or so articles and 5 emails (building an
auto-responder) each DAY -- 7 days per week. This was in addition to my daily
BenSettle.com email, client work (I was on retainer and working like a machine on their
stuff too) and ajoint venture that included me writing a lot. It was also the time when I
learned MORE about how to write emails than any other edu-ma-cation I ever received.
I was basically forced to learn how to write really fast, with as little editing needed as
possible, and that was also persuasive. I learned a few cool tricks for shortening it, too.
Like, for example, my forum tactic (where I look at the forum threads with the highest
üew count and adapt those thread titles to my subject lines and article titles), looking at
stories in forums of people who suffered the problem I was solving and using those stories
o

EIVÍAIL PIAYERS COMI\,IANDMENT II
Thous Shalt Read 66The Ernail Players Playbook»
A Bare Minirnurn Of 10 Tirnes!
It always amaztes me how someone can buy an expensive book or info product and
only read it one or two times. It doesn't make any sense. Since you're patrng $100, $200,
$500, $1,000 (or more) for a course on copywriting or marketing or whatever it is you do,
doesn't it make sense to read it over and over and over again -- milking out every single
secret you can? I like to cover the stuff I buy at least ten times in a row -- minimum. And
that is what I am asking you to do, too.Just pick it up and work through it - highlighting
and underlining and making notes along the way -- until you're read it ten times. It's not a
long book and this should be doable even with the other assigned tasks I'm gü"g you in
this issue. After that, read it at least once a year. This is one of the best ways of mastering
a subject you will ever see. Besides, you won't get all the gold out of an info product on
the lst, 2nd or even 3rd reading. But you'll be amazed at what comes out on the 6th, 7th,
even the 10th reading. And by the 15th reading... you'll O\ /l{ the info in that book
(probably know it better than I do!)And when it comes time to prove yourself (each day,
when you sit down to write your emails), you'lIbe ready. You'lIbe prepared. And, you'll
be equipped with the knowledge you need.
EI\,ÍAIL PLI\YERS C OMI\,IANDMENT III
Ihou Shalt Find Your Favorite Copywriter
And Copy Out One Of His Ads (By Hand) Each Day!
Yes, I know I am on record sayrng emails are not "copywriting" -- they are mostly
based on your personality and the way you talk. But, just because writing emails is not tire
sarne as copywriting, that doesn't mean it stilt isn't copy. And this hand copying out ads is
something the late copywriter Gury Halbert used to insist his students do to get a
"neurological imprint" of what it's like to write world class copy. After doing some
amateur research on the human brain, I do see why this works. And, I can definitely say, it
worked to help me get up to snuff lar faster than a lot of other people I know who were
getting started at about the same time I did. Plus, as even Stephen Kirg said when he was
writing "Dreamcatchers" via pen and paper in a hospital room: Wriüng by hand gets you

EMAIL PIAYERS COMI\4AhIDMENT Iv
Thou Shalt Listen To One High Rating
Talk Radio Show Episode Per Day!
Now we're getting down to where the rubber meets the dough. One of the best
things you can do for your email writing is listen to talk radio shows that have high
ratings. My favorite talk radio guy is Michael Savage. Raw. Opinionated. Controversial.
Brilliant at pitching his books on air, while still delivering content and entertainment. Plus,
he doesn't dance to anyone else's tune. (A great example of how email writers should be.)
He's also a master storyteller, too. There are VERY few people who can tell stories like he
can. This dude can take the most ordinary bland, "plain vanilla" things that happen
during a day and turn them into a captivating, riveting story. This is a great skill you can
at least partially learn via "osmosis" while listening to his show. What's that? You can't
find any good talk radio to listen to? Fear ye not, just go to this site:
http r I I strearningradioguide. corn/ radio-shows-on-ainphp

I learned this originally while listening to an interview Gury Bencivenga gave for
Ken McCarthy's "System Club" members. He called it his "One a day marketing
ütamin." And what he does is, he reads one ad per day and analyzes it trying to think of
ways to make it even better. This is a great exercise. I.{o ad is perfect, after all. And by
training your brain to look at great ads and find their flaws so you can improve on them,
you can't help but get better (and fast)yourself No*, what I am commandingyou to do

EMAIL PI"AYERS COMI\,IANDMENT X
Thou Shalt Read Thy Sparn!
And now we're down to the final peg in the email-mastery ladder. Listen, several
years ago a freebie seeker wrote me complaining about how he didn't want to buy
anything and only came to my site looking for free information. He said he could learn
copywriting (what I was focusing on teaching at the time) at his local community college
and didn't wish to see sales pitches for anything to buy. He only wanted free information.
What did I tell the tittle snowflake? I told him that, instead of whining about the sales
pitches he gets in his inbox, to quite being such a pussy and READ them -- the good, bad
or fugly. For one thing he might learn something. Heck -- he may even want to BUY
something. And when that happens, to make sure he keeps it and dissects it line by line.
Then, figure out exactly what it did to sell him... and then start using it in HIS ads.
And dear Email Player, I am now gürg you the same adüce. Read that spam. (Although
if you're worried about viruses, etc, like I am, just have your email program have images
turned off by default, and never click on any of the links -- if you want to see a site, copy
and paste the URL into Google first, then see if it's legit.) Ary*uy, it'll be one of the best
educations you get. Plus, unlike the community college... it won't cost ya a dime.
And there ya have it.
A 30 day plan to become a top notch world class email marketer.
Hey, I'm not saying it will be easy (that's kinda the point, babycakes). But if you
truly have what it takes, you'll do it with a smile. I am not asking you to do anything I
have not done (and, in many of these cases, STILL do). So this ain't theory or nonsense.
It's real, it works, and it is something I believe anyone can do if you muster up the self
discipline and manage your time prope.ly. (If you suck at managing your time, I
recommend reading Dan Kennedy's "NO BS Time Management" book -- one of the

about this client I helped with his business..." Then talk

readers. That sells them on you without being blatant about

can trust (thus daily demonstration of your skills via

I've been using the following kind of email a lot in the weight loss niche over the
past several months. One reason why is, I don't want to spend a lot of time writing the
emails to that list. And the second reason is, it makes writing emails extremely fast and
simple, and I don't have to do a whole lot of thinking. (It takes me about 10 minutes to
write this kind of email). So what do I mean by the "comment on an article" email? Well,
it's basically exactly like it sounds: You find an article online (of offline, it matters not --
you could even do this with a TV show or radio show or any kind of opinion, scholarly or
editorial content, really) and give your "take" on what it says. Then, segue into why the
reader should buy your product. Very simple. Very quick. And, very eflective when done
correctly. Below is an example. You will see, you can use this method to sell products that
